source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider external secret storage

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Consider external secret storage
input_row.description: Configure	Image	Provenance	for	your	deployment.
input_row.rationale: Kubernetes	supports	plugging	in	provenance	rules	to	accept	or	reject	the	images	in	your deployments.	You	could	configure	such	rules	to	ensure	that	only	approved	images	are deployed	in	the	cluster. Impact: You	need	to	regularly	maintain	your	provenance	configuration	based	on	container	image updates.
input_row.audit: Review	the	pod	definitions	in	your	cluster	and	verify	that	image	provenance	is	configured as	appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow	the	Kubernetes	documentation	and	setup	image	provenance. Default	Value: By	default,	image	provenance	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/image-provenance.md 3. https://hub.docker.com/r/dnurmi/anchore-toolbox/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22888 239	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security Version	7 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security 240	|	P a g e 5.6 General Policies These	policies	relate	to	general	cluster	management	topics,	like	namespace	best	practices and	policies	applied	to	pod	objects	in	the	cluster.
input_row.impact: You	need	to	regularly	maintain	your	provenance	configuration	based	on	container	image updates.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/image-provenance.md 3. https://hub.docker.com/r/dnurmi/anchore-toolbox/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22888 239	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control "Consider external secret storage" regarding configuring image provenance for Kubernetes deployments involves verifying the configuration of image provenance rules in the Kubernetes pod definitions. These rules ensure that only approved images are deployed. This is a more nuanced configuration that falls outside the scope of typical API-exposed settings and requires a more detailed assessment of Kubernetes policies, potentially involving reviewing YAML configuration files or consulting with administrators maintaining these settings. As a result, this requires human judgment and is not directly verifiable through Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, or the Alibaba Cloud CLI.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **Review Kubernetes Pod Definitions:**
   - Navigate to the Kubernetes cluster where the workloads are deployed.
   - Collect all pod definition files (often YAML or JSON formatted) in use within your cluster.

2. **Verify Image Provenance Configuration:**
   - Open each pod definition file and look for policies related to image provenance.
   - Confirm that an "ImagePolicyWebhook" is configured if applicable, referencing an accepted list of image signatures or a proper CI/CD pipeline.

3. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - Check that provenance rules are aligned with organizational policies, ensuring only authorized images are runnable.
   - Verify if the ImagePolicyWebhook is consistently applied across all significant deployments (production, staging, etc.).

4. **Validate Against Company Standards:**
   - Compare the configuration against organizational standards documentation to confirm compliance.
   - Ensure regular updates and maintenance of the provenance configuration files to reflect any updates in approved images.

5. **Consult Kubernetes Documentation:**
   - Refer to [Kubernetes Image Policy Webhook](https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook) for setting up image provenance.
   - Consider best practices from community forums or additional security tools that integrate with Kubernetes like the Anchore Toolbox for additional compliance verification.

Given that the control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters as noted in the references, any adjustments would be manual and require human validation.
